record_identity.title: Strategic continuity salience and dormant-workstream resurfacing coverage

summary: Records the retrospective Caelestis coverage for FM-0003 and PROP-0006. IDENTITY-001-SUP-01 already defines long-arc salience, delegated salience, a recency-bias constraint, dormant-but-relevant posture, and cautious re-surfacing.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: VIGIL should show that strategic continuity retrieval is governed by existing salience doctrine rather than leaving the failure falsely marked uncovered.

source_records.deployment_context: Canonical Caelestis main-branch corpus reviewed for VIGIL repair reconciliation.
source_records.source_context: Defines long-arc salience, recency-bias constraints, dormant-but-relevant salience, re-surfacing conditions, and corresponding failure states.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary current-corpus evidence for the VIGIL coverage assessment.

system_context.system_type: CAM governance corpus / retrospective coverage record
system_context.interface_surface: failure, proposal, patch, schema, validator, and generated-index interfaces

change_classification.change_scope: Pre-existing canonical CAM/Caelestis doctrine and its failure-to-repair crosswalk
change_classification.implementation_level: pre-existing canonical CAM doctrine; VIGIL crosswalk only

change_details.change_summary: This retrospective patch note identifies the actual pre-existing CAM control content and links it to the ecosystem failure. It does not represent VIGIL repository administration as a patch.
change_details.doctrine_change: No new Caelestis doctrine was created. Existing canonical provisions were identified and linked to the relevant VIGIL records.

implementation_verification.verification_method: Direct review of canonical Caelestis main at the recorded commit, reciprocal VIGIL linkage, validator execution, and generated-index rebuild.
implementation_verification.verified_by: VIGIL repository automation and maintainer review
implementation_verification.verification_result: implemented in VIGIL and confirmed against current Caelestis corpus

impact_summary.intended_effect: VIGIL should show that strategic continuity retrieval is governed by existing salience doctrine rather than leaving the failure falsely marked uncovered.
impact_summary.known_limitations: External runtime adoption and behavioural conformance remain unverified.

remaining_work: External runtime adoption and behavioural conformance remain unverified.

cam_internal.governance_layer: retrospective CAM corpus coverage / failure-to-doctrine crosswalk
cam_internal.routing_note: No CAM instrument was amended on this VIGIL record date. The patch note records pre-existing Caelestis coverage and its effective origin; VIGIL itself is not the patch target.

repair_provenance.repair_basis: Existing canonical Caelestis coverage was identified, verified, and linked without amending the doctrine.
